name: Houston Food Bank
description: The largest food bank in the region, distributing food through more than 1,500 community partner agencies across 18 southeast Texas counties, including on-site distribution.
eligibility: Open to all residents in need; no documentation required for most distributions

name: Interfaith Ministries for Greater Houston - Meals on Wheels
description: Delivers home-cooked meals to homebound seniors and disabled adults across Harris and Galveston counties, along with weekend meals and pet food for clients' animals through the Animeals program.
eligibility: Homebound seniors (60+) and disabled adults in Harris/Galveston/Montgomery/Liberty/Chambers counties

name: Bread of Life Inc
description: Provides emergency food assistance and meals for unhoused individuals and families in the Houston area, alongside broader emergency service support.
eligibility: Open to individuals and families in need

name: Salvation Army - Center of Hope for Men
description: Emergency and transitional shelter providing beds, meals, and program support for single men experiencing homelessness, with case management toward permanent housing.
eligibility: Single men experiencing homelessness; intake Mon-Fri 8:00am-5:00pm

name: Salvation Army Family Residence for Women and Children
description: Emergency shelter providing beds, meals, and case management for women and children experiencing homelessness.
eligibility: Women and children experiencing homelessness

name: Star of Hope - Men's Development Center
description: Christ-centered emergency shelter and residential program for homeless men, offering meals, case management, and recovery support programs. Maximum stay of 30 days unless an extension is granted.
eligibility: Adult men experiencing homelessness; intake Mon-Fri 8:00am-3:30pm

name: Star of Hope - Cornerstone Community (Women & Family)
description: Christ-centered emergency shelter for homeless women and families with children, providing meals, on-site daycare, and programs toward stable housing.
eligibility: Women and families with children experiencing homelessness

name: Magnificat House
description: Provides emergency and transitional housing along with meal support for individuals experiencing homelessness in Houston.
eligibility: Open to individuals experiencing homelessness; residents contribute to household upkeep

name: Covenant House Texas
description: Emergency shelter and transitional housing specifically for homeless and trafficked youth, offering crisis intervention, case management, education, and job-readiness support.
eligibility: Youth ages 18-24; open 24/7 walk-in intake

name: Mission of Yahweh
description: Emergency shelter providing housing, meals, and support services for women and their children experiencing homelessness.
eligibility: Women and children experiencing homelessness

name: Santa Maria Hostel
description: Residential shelter and recovery program for women, including those with substance use needs, offering housing alongside treatment services.
eligibility: Women, including those seeking residential recovery services

name: The Beacon Day Center
description: Day shelter for people experiencing homelessness offering hot meals, showers, laundry facilities, and case management. Does not provide overnight beds.
eligibility: Open to all people experiencing homelessness; no overnight beds

name: Lone Star Legal Aid - Houston Office
description: Provides free civil legal representation and advice to low-income Texans on housing, eviction defense, family law, consumer, and public benefits matters. Accepts walk-in, phone, and online applications.
eligibility: Household income generally under 125% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines; some special projects have broader eligibility

name: AVDA (Aid to Victims of Domestic Abuse)
description: Provides free legal representation in family law matters — including protective orders, divorce, child custody, and child support — specifically for survivors of domestic violence in Harris and Fort Bend counties.
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence in Harris/Fort Bend county; protective order services have no income restriction

name: BakerRipley Utility Assistance Program (CEAP)
description: Helps eligible households in Harris, Brazoria, and Galveston counties pay electricity, gas, or propane utility bills through the Comprehensive Energy Assistance Program, funded by the Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs.
eligibility: Household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level; must reside in Harris, Brazoria, or Galveston County

name: Christian Community Service Center (CCSC) Central
description: Provides rent, utility, and food assistance along with counseling, adoption, and immigration services through an appointment-based intake process.
eligibility: Income-based; appointment required, scheduled on a first-call basis

name: Texas Rent Relief Program
description: Statewide program providing emergency rental and utility assistance to eligible Texas households experiencing financial hardship, administered by the Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs.
eligibility: Texas residents experiencing financial hardship affecting ability to pay rent or utilities; income limits apply

name: BakerRipley Early Childhood Development Center
description: Free, federally-funded Head Start program providing early childhood education for preschool-age children, with a curriculum aligned to Texas educational standards.
eligibility: Children ages 3-5; income-eligible families

name: BakerRipley Head Start - Sharpview
description: Free Early Head Start and Head Start preschool program serving infants through preschool-age children.
eligibility: Children ages 0-4; income-eligible families

name: BakerRipley Head Start - Hiram Clarke
description: Free Head Start preschool program serving young children in the Hiram Clarke area of Houston.
eligibility: Children ages 0-4; income-eligible families

name: Workforce Solutions Childcare Financial Assistance
description: Provides subsidized childcare assistance for low-to-moderate income working families, covering up to 12 months with the option for annual recertification.
eligibility: Household income up to 85% of the State Median Income; parent must be working or in school

name: The Harris Center for Mental Health and IDD - Crisis and Access Line
description: 24/7 crisis phone line staffed by counselors for anyone experiencing a behavioral health crisis, alongside outpatient clinics offering medication management and counseling on a sliding scale.
eligibility: Open to all Harris County residents; services not denied based on inability to pay

name: Montrose Center
description: LGBTQ+-affirming counseling center offering individual, couples, and group therapy, substance use treatment, and case management. Discounted fees available for uninsured clients.
eligibility: Open to all; LGBTQ+ affirming; discounted fees for uninsured clients

name: Houston Area Women's Center
description: Provides counseling, case management, and a 24-hour hotline for survivors of domestic and sexual violence.
eligibility: Survivors of domestic or sexual violence; hotline open to all

name: Coalition for the Homeless of Houston/Harris County
description: Coordinates the region's homeless response system (The Way Home) and provides referrals to emergency shelters, housing programs, and other homeless services across Harris, Fort Bend, and Montgomery counties.
eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness seeking referrals to services

name: SEARCH Homeless Services
description: Day center and outreach program offering case management, meals, and a jail in-reach program to help people experiencing homelessness access services and housing.
eligibility: Open to people experiencing homelessness

name: United Way of Greater Houston 211 Helpline
description: 24/7 information and referral hotline connecting callers to local social services, including food, housing, healthcare, and financial assistance programs.
eligibility: Free; open to anyone

name: Care Connection Aging and Disability Resource Center
description: Provides free, confidential information and referral assistance connecting older adults, veterans, and individuals with disabilities to services, benefits, and community supports in Harris County.
eligibility: Older adults, veterans, and individuals of all ages with disabilities in Harris County
